task: According to the official CDC guidance, is doxycycline recommended for routine treatment of acute bronchitis in otherwise healthy adults?
answer: No. Doxycycline is not recommended for routine treatment of acute bronchitis in otherwise healthy adults.

steps.text: I should check the CDC guidance on acute bronchitis and routine antibiotics.

steps.input: CDC acute bronchitis routine antibiotics guidance otherwise healthy adults

steps.text: Search results indicate CDC outpatient guidance discourages routine antibiotic treatment for uncomplicated acute bronchitis in otherwise healthy adults.
Doxycycline is an antibiotic, so this is probably enough to answer.
The CDC outpatient page says routine treatment of uncomplicated acute bronchitis with antibiotics is not recommended, regardless of cough duration.
No. Doxycycline is not recommended for routine treatment of acute bronchitis in otherwise healthy adults.
